info.title: Proof-of-Reserves Verifier API
info.summary: Interactive challenge/response API for privacy-preserving proof of native-coin reserves.
info.description:
An agent proves it controls one or more wallets whose **combined** native-coin balance is at least a threshold T, at three unpredictable finalized blocks, **without revealing balances, addresses, or the number of wallets**.

Flow:
1. `POST /v1/challenges` — ask for a challenge (the verifier picks a random nonce and 3 recent block numbers).
2. Run the `prover` locally to produce a response (3 RISC Zero receipts + TLSNotary header attestations + an owner signature). Your key never touches this service. Proving is CPU by default; the Docker `prover` auto-uses an NVIDIA GPU when run with `--gpus all` (≥16 GB VRAM) — see the guide.
3. `POST /v1/challenges/{id}/response` — submit the response; get a verdict.
4. `GET /v1/challenges/{id}` — poll state / verdict / settle progress.

Registration (`GET /v1/register`) and the human/agent guide are described here too. See also the machine-readable spec at `/v1/openapi.json` and the MCP server (`por-mcp.mjs`) for LLM-agent integration.

tags.name: registration
tags.description: How to register an agent so the verifier will authenticate it

get.description: Human/machine-readable service descriptor: version, endpoints, supported chains, notary hint, and links.

200.description: The OpenAPI 3.1 spec for this service

get.description: The chains reserves can be proven on. `selector` is what you pass as `chain_id` when requesting a challenge; `resolved_chain_id` is the effective chain (in testnet mode a mainnet selector resolves to its paired testnet). The proof commits `resolved_chain_id`.

200.description: Supported chains for the current mode

get.description: The verifier does not custody keys and cannot register on your behalf. Registration is a one-time on-chain `IdentityRegistry.register(agentURI)` transaction sent **from your own wallet** on Base Sepolia — `msg.sender` becomes the agent owner, and that owner is exactly what this verifier authenticates against (`ecrecover(owner_sig) == ownerOf(agentId)`). This endpoint returns everything you need to build that transaction (contract address, chain, ABI, calldata template, metadata template). The easiest ways to actually send it: the MCP `register_agent` tool, the `register-agent.mjs` helper, or a browser wallet on the `/prove` page's Register step.

post.description: Look up the agent's registered owner, draw a random nonce, pin the chain's finalized head, and derive 3 recent, unpredictable, finalized block numbers spread across a multi-day window. Returns the challenge to prove.

400.description: Bad request (missing/invalid `agent_id`/`threshold`, or unsupported `chain_id` — body carries a `supported` list)

404.description: Agent not found in the registry

post.summary: Submit a response to a challenge
post.description: Verify all 3 RISC Zero receipts + their TLSNotary bindings, cross-check they answer this challenge, authenticate the owner signature against the registry owner, and return a verdict. The body carries three ~0.5 MB CBOR receipts — stream it (`--data-binary @response.json`), do not inline it. A challenge can be answered once.

200.description: Verdict (verified or rejected). Note: a rejected verdict is still HTTP 200 with `verdict:"rejected"` and a `reason`.

get.description: Current state, the verdict/reason once submitted, and — after a verified verdict when settle is enabled — the zkVerify/marketplace settle progress under `kurier`.

agent_id.description: Marketplace agent id — the ERC-721 token id, hex (`0x16ec`) or decimal (`5868`). Must be registered.

threshold.description: Minimum combined reserves in wei of the native coin. Decimal string, JSON number, or `0x`-hex string. u128 range.

chain_id.description: Chain selector (default 1). See `GET /v1/chains`. In testnet mode a mainnet selector resolves to its paired testnet.

Challenge.description: A verifier-issued challenge. Signed fields (nonce, agent, threshold, chain, block set) are bound by the owner signature.

chain_id.description: The resolved (effective) chain id, committed in each proof's journal.

head_block.description: Finalized head pinned at issue time.

expires_at.description: Unix seconds — challenge expires ~1h after issue.

Response.description: A prover's response: 3 self-contained proof bundles + the owner signature over the challenge. Produced by the `prover` locally.

owner_sig.description: 0x-hex, 65 bytes (r‖s‖v), EIP-191 over the challenge digest; must recover to the registry owner.

bundles.description: Exactly 3 bundles, one per challenged block.

Bundle.description: A zkVerify/Kurier-shaped proof bundle for one block.

publicSignals.description: 0x-hex guest journal bytes (block_hash, threshold, chain_id, debug, challenge_nonce, agent_id, block_number, …). No balance or address.

tlsnPresentation.description: base64 TLSNotary header attestation. Required unless the verifier runs with POR_ALLOW_NO_PRESENTATION.

kurier.description: zkVerify/marketplace settle progress (only when settle is enabled and the verdict is verified).

testnet.description: Whether the service runs in testnet mode.

resolved_chain_id.description: Effective chain id committed in the proof.

rpc_host.description: The TLSNotary-attested header host bound to this chain.

image_id.description: The RISC Zero guest image_id the verifier accepts (0x-hex, LE words). Your prover must produce receipts under exactly this id.

notary_hint.description: Whether a TLSNotary presentation is required, and where to find the NOTARY_ADDR to use.

notary_addr.description: The live TLSNotary host:port to set as NOTARY_ADDR (the operator's public notary endpoint), or null if none is published. Read this dynamically — the value can move between notary redeploys.

tooling.description: Where to obtain and run the prover / MCP tooling. Docker images are self-contained (no source checkout); the MCP server lives in the repo.

prover_cli.description: Headless Docker prover for AI agents (image, pull, run).

prover_web.description: Browser-wallet Docker app for humans (image, run).

mcp_server.description: MCP server for AI agents (repo path, install, transport, prerequisites).

metadata_template.description: Recommended AgentCard metadata; base64-encode as a data: URI for agentURI.

tools.description: The easiest ways to actually send the transaction.

note.description: Why msg.sender must be the wallet you'll authorize challenges with.

supported.description: On an unsupported chain_id, the selectors this service accepts.
